Title: Recycling Upstream Redox Enzymes Expands the Regioselectivity of Cycloaddition in Pseudo-Aspidosperma Alkaloid Biosynthesis
Journal: Journal of the American Chemical Society

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Monoterpene indole alkaloid pseudo-aspidosperma/iboga scaffold cyclization branch from dehydrosecodine
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Reaction pH; pH 7.5 favors Ψ-tabersonine branch with TiDPAS1 and PAS, whereas pH 9.5 favors coronaridine branch with TiDPAS2
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cyclase involved in formal cycloaddition of dehydrosecodine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro coupled enzyme assays; transient reconstitution in Nicotiana benthamiana; trapping/isolation of unstable intermediate with NaBH4/NaBD4; LC-MS/MS; NMR; CD spectroscopy; deuterium-labeling with NADPD
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Monoterpene indole alkaloids with pharmaceutical relevance; coronaridine is a precursor to ibogaine and Ψ-tabersonine is proposed precursor to 20-epi-ibophyllidine
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TiCorS releases unstable 16-carbomethoxycleaviminium intermediate, enabling interception by upstream redox enzymes and alternative scaffold formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms branch-defining cyclization intermediate that leads to target alkaloid scaffolds
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TiCorS cyclizes dehydrosecodine to 16-carbomethoxycleaviminium. 2. Subsequent redox processing redirects this intermediate to (-)-coronaridine, (+)-Ψ-tabersonine, or (+)-Ψ-vincadifformine. 3. Product outcome depends on reductase isoform and assay pH.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves for reconstitution
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Determines cyclization scaffold and enables formation of rare pseudo-aspidosperma alkaloids when coupled to PAS and TiDPAS1
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome mining of Tabernanthe iboga; no accession reported for transcriptome in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes dehydrosecodine to unstable 16-carbomethoxycleaviminium, which can proceed to different alkaloid scaffolds after downstream redox steps
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Product selectivity is condition dependent and may require nonphysiological pH for coronaridine formation in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful branch-switch catalyst for engineering alternative monoterpene indole alkaloid scaffolds in heterologous hosts when paired with specific DPAS/PAS enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: TiDPAS1; TiDPAS2; TiPAS1; TiPAS2; TiPAS3; CrPAS

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Upstream redox step and recycled reductive branch leading to pseudo-aspidosperma alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: pH 7.5 favored Ψ-tabersonine formation with TiCorS and PAS
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro enzyme assays with specific isoforms; deuterium labeling using pro-(R)-NADPD; reconstitution in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Produces rare pseudo-aspidosperma alkaloids of pharmacological interest
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts upstream to reduce precondylocarpine acetate and is recycled downstream to perform 1,2-reduction of TiCorS product, enabling Ψ-tabersonine/Ψ-vincadifformine formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for downstream branch redirection via reductive interception of TiCorS intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TiDPAS1 supports formation of (+)-Ψ-tabersonine at pH 7.5 with TiCorS and PAS. 2. Deuterium incorporation showed TiDPAS1 contributes an additional reduction step beyond its upstream role. 3. Together with PAS it also enables formation of (+)-Ψ-vincadifformine.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for branch selection with TiCorS
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves for reconstitution
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes pseudo-aspidosperma scaffold formation through 1,2-reduction of TiCorS-derived intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces precondylocarpine acetate in the upstream pathway and reduces 16-carbomethoxycleaviminium to 16-carbomethoxycleavamine in the recycled branch
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Isoform-dependent selectivity; may over-reduce intermediate to produce Ψ-vincadifformine
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be paired with TiCorS and PAS to engineer pseudo-aspidosperma alkaloid production in heterologous systems

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Dihydroprecondylocarpine acetate; reductively processed intermediate leading to (-)-coronaridine
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Upstream redox step and recycled reductive branch leading to iboga alkaloid scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: pH 9.5 favored coronaridine formation with TiCorS
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro enzyme assays comparing TiDPAS isoforms; product profiling by LC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Coronaridine is precursor to antiaddiction agent ibogaine
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Proposed to favor 1,4-reduction of TiCorS-derived intermediate under higher pH, directing flux to coronaridine
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for downstream branch redirection to coronaridine
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TiDPAS2 with TiCorS favored (-)-coronaridine formation at pH 9.5. 2. PAS was not required for coronaridine production. 3. The data support isoform-specific 1,4-reduction driving iboga scaffold formation.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for branch selection with TiCorS
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes coronaridine formation rather than pseudo-aspidosperma products
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces precondylocarpine acetate upstream and is proposed to perform 1,4-reduction of the TiCorS product, enabling cyclization to coronaridine
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Requires higher pH in vitro; coronaridine not reconstituted in Nicotiana benthamiana under tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering iboga alkaloid branch when combined with TiCorS under suitable conditions

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Precondylocarpine acetate; oxidized intermediate leading to (+)-Ψ-tabersonine and (+)-Ψ-vincadifformine
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Upstream oxidation step and recycled oxidation branch in pseudo-aspidosperma biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: pH 7.5 supports pseudo-aspidosperma formation with TiDPAS1 and TiCorS
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro coupled assays; heterologous reconstitution in Nicotiana benthamiana; feeding of reduced intermediate 10; LC-MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pseudo-aspidosperma alkaloids are specialized bioactive alkaloids; Ψ-tabersonine proposed precursor of 20-epi-ibophyllidine
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts upstream on stemmadenine acetate and is recycled downstream to reoxidize 16-carbomethoxycleavamine, priming cyclization to pseudo-aspidosperma products
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for downstream oxidation that enables target scaffold formation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TiPAS1 can support (+)-Ψ-tabersonine formation with TiDPAS1 and TiCorS. 2. PAS activity is required for pseudo-aspidosperma products but not for coronaridine. 3. TiPAS1 also supports formation of (+)-Ψ-vincadifformine under appropriate conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point in recycled redox module
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves for reconstitution
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables pseudo-aspidosperma scaffold formation by oxidation after TiDPAS1 reduction
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream and recycled downstream oxidation step
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes stemmadenine acetate to precondylocarpine acetate and oxidizes 16-carbomethoxycleavamine to a cyclization-competent intermediate en route to Ψ-tabersonine
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Homologue selectivity seen in vitro may be obscured in planta by endogenous Nicotiana oxidase activity
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be deployed with TiCorS and TiDPAS1 for heterologous production of rare alkaloid scaffolds

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Precondylocarpine acetate; oxidized intermediate leading to (+)-Ψ-tabersonine and (+)-Ψ-vincadifformine
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Upstream oxidation step and recycled oxidation branch in pseudo-aspidosperma biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: pH 7.5 supports pseudo-aspidosperma formation with TiDPAS1 and TiCorS
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro coupled assays; product profiling by LC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pseudo-aspidosperma alkaloids are specialized bioactive alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of three T. iboga PAS homologues supporting the recycled oxidation step for pseudo-aspidosperma scaffold formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for downstream oxidation that enables target scaffold formation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TiPAS2 supported (+)-Ψ-tabersonine formation with TiDPAS1 and TiCorS. 2. PAS family enzymes were required for pseudo-aspidosperma products but not coronaridine.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point in recycled redox module
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports formation of pseudo-aspidosperma alkaloids in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream and recycled downstream oxidation step
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes stemmadenine acetate to precondylocarpine acetate and supports oxidation of reduced TiCorS-derived intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative PAS isoform for alkaloid engineering

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TiPAS3 supported (+)-Ψ-tabersonine formation with TiDPAS1 and TiCorS. 2. PAS family enzymes were required for pseudo-aspidosperma products but not coronaridine.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes stemmadenine acetate to precondylocarpine acetate and supports oxidation of reduced TiCorS-derived intermediate

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Precondylocarpine acetate; oxidized intermediate leading to (+)-Ψ-tabersonine
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Upstream oxidation step and recycled oxidation branch in heterologous pseudo-aspidosperma biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: pH 7.5 supports pseudo-aspidosperma formation with TiDPAS1 and TiCorS
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro coupled assays; deuterium-labeling assays
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pseudo-aspidosperma alkaloids are specialized bioactive alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Heterologous PAS from C. roseus could substitute for T. iboga PAS homologues in vitro for Ψ-tabersonine formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for downstream oxidation that enables target scaffold formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports heterologous in vitro formation of Ψ-tabersonine when combined with TiDPAS1 and TiCorS
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream and recycled downstream oxidation step
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes stemmadenine acetate to precondylocarpine acetate and can reoxidize reduced TiCorS-derived intermediate en route to Ψ-tabersonine
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Selectivity differences observed in vitro were not retained in planta because of endogenous N. benthamiana oxidase activity
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful heterologous PAS for pathway reconstruction

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Monoterpene indole alkaloid tabersonine cyclization branch from dehydrosecodine
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: pH 7.5 with PAS and TiDPAS1 favored tabersonine formation
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cyclase involved in formal cycloaddition of dehydrosecodine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro enzyme assays; transient reconstitution in Nicotiana benthamiana; deuterium-labeling with NADPD
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Tabersonine is precursor to anticancer drugs vinblastine and vincristine
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a pathway-defining comparator demonstrating alternative scaffold outcomes from dehydrosecodine
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TiTabS converts dehydrosecodine to (-)-tabersonine. 2. Under pH 7.5 with PAS/TiDPAS1, tabersonine formation was favored in the TiTabS branch. 3. Deuterium labeling showed only the expected upstream reduction contribution.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves for reconstitution
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces tabersonine rather than iboga or pseudo-aspidosperma scaffolds
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes dehydrosecodine to (-)-tabersonine via formal [4+2] cycloaddition
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful comparator or stackable branch enzyme for engineering tabersonine-derived alkaloids
